name: time
import: import time  # OR: from time import sleep, ticks_ms, ticks_diff, etc.
description: Time-related functions. Subset of CPython's time module with MicroPython-specific additions for embedded timing.
differences_from_cpython: No timezone support. No datetime module built-in.
MicroPython adds ticks_ms(), ticks_us(), ticks_diff(), ticks_add() for embedded timing.
time.time() on ESP32 returns seconds since boot (NOT Unix epoch).

functions.name: time.sleep

parameters.name: seconds
parameters.description: Seconds to sleep. Float for sub-second.

functions.note: Blocking. For non-blocking delays, use ticks_ms() + ticks_diff().

functions.name: time.sleep_ms
functions.note: More efficient than sleep() for millisecond delays.

functions.name: time.sleep_us
functions.note: Minimum sleep is ~30µs. For shorter delays, use busy-wait with machine.bit() or NOP.

functions.name: time.time

returns.description: Seconds since power-on or last hard reset (ESP32).

functions.note: ON ESP32: NOT Unix epoch. Use RTC for calendar time. Use time.time() for elapsed time.

functions.name: time.ticks_ms
functions.note: USE WITH ticks_diff() to handle wrap correctly. NEVER compare ticks values directly!

functions.name: time.ticks_us
functions.note: USE WITH ticks_diff() to handle wrap correctly.

functions.name: time.ticks_cpu

returns.description: CPU cycle counter. Highest resolution, wraps quickly.

functions.note: Use for micro-benchmarks. Not portable across CPU speeds.

functions.name: time.ticks_diff

returns.description: Signed difference, correct even across wrap.

functions.note: ALWAYS use this to compare ticks. ticks1 - ticks2 is WRONG (fails at wrap).
functions.WRONG: if ticks_ms() - last > 1000:  # WRONG! Breaks at wrap!

parameters.name: secs
parameters.description: Seconds since epoch. None = use RTC time.

returns.description: (year, month, mday, hour, minute, second, weekday, yearday). weekday=0-6 (Mon=0).

functions.name: time.localtime
functions.returns: Same as gmtime (no timezone difference on MicroPython).

functions.name: time.mktime
functions.note: MicroPython epoch is 2000-01-01, NOT 1970-01-01 as in CPython!
